results.rendered_html: uj-jayinī f. the city Oujein (the Gk. Ὀζήνη, a city so called in Avanti or Mālava, formerly the capital of Vikramāditya; it is one of the seven sacred cities of the Hindūs, and the first meridian of their geographers, from which they calculate longitude; the modern Oujein is about a mile south of the ancient city),  Hit. ;  Megh. ;  Rājat.  &c.

results.rendered_html: ujjaya(yi)nī N. of a city, the modern Oujein in Mālvā, the capital of Vikramāditya. It is one of the seven sacred cities of the Hindus (cf. avaṃti), and the first meridian of their geographers from which they calculate longitude; saudhotsaṃgapraṇayavimukho mā sma bhūrujjayinyāḥ  Me. 27.
